answers: Start drums: learn basic beat (kick on 1 and 3, snare on 2 and 4, hi-hat eighths). Practice with a metronome.
Beginner tips: start slow, focus on timing over speed, use practice pad for quiet practice, and play along to songs.
Drum kit options: acoustic (real sound, loud), electronic (quiet, versatile). Roland or Alesis for good electronic kits.
Drumming benefits: great exercise, stress relief, improves coordination, and makes you the backbone of any band.
